label: offensive
rationale: 'pussy', 'coward' - crude personal insults aimed at Uhuru Kenyatta as an individual politician, no protected characteristic.

label: neither
rationale: Cynical commentary on a named politician's (Oguda/Wandai) opportunism and poverty; criticism of individuals, no group attack.

text: RT @Lightcasttvke: "IG Kanja ni flower girl, nderitu Mureithi anatumiwa kuhangaisha Wakikuyu." ~ Gachagua
label: neither
rationale: Quotes Gachagua's claim that state actors are being used to harass Kikuyus - framing is political grievance about state targeting of a community, not an attack on the community itself; RT with no endorsement of incitement.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyus are ones who suffer more machafuko ikianzanga so waaje fujo' - holds the Kikuyu community collectively responsible for starting ethnic violence, attributing a negative characteristic to the group as a whole under NCIC standard.

label: neither
rationale: Personal jibe at an individual ('you still are in pain like Wamunyoro') with no group attack.

label: neither
rationale: 'Kalonzo is stingy af' - mild personal criticism of a named politician, no group characteristic transferred.

label: neither
rationale: Observational political critique noting Murkomen celebrated Kalenjin deaths while Kalenjins avoid protests; criticises politicians and community political behaviour analytically, no contempt or dehumanisation.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kwani ni wakikuyu tu wako na shida na William ... Presidency hamtaona' - declares Kikuyus collectively will be denied the presidency, attributing collective political grievance and imposing group-level political exclusion under NCIC standard.

label: offensive
rationale: 'mjinga', 'mpumbavu mkubwa' - crude personal insults directed at Ruto as an individual; 'he has to kill 55 million Kenyans' reads as hyperbolic political rhetoric, not a credible violence call.

label: hate
rationale: 'America won't survive if we allow African-born socialists to rule us' - declares a national-origin group categorically unfit to hold office, collective exclusion on ethnic/national-origin grounds.

label: neither
rationale: Ironic self-description of a 'tolerant' community listing a miscellany of groups including hostile labels; overall framing is sardonic boasting of tolerance rather than an attack on any specific group.

label: neither
rationale: 'Democrats are the most corrupt people' - harsh political criticism of a political party, not a protected characteristic.

label: neither
rationale: Condemns government killings of 'Olk people' as inconsequential - expressing outrage at state violence, not attacking a group; 'goons' targets political actors, not an ethnic community.

label: hate
rationale: 'Waluuuuhyaaaa next ni nyinyi' combined with 'shida yenyu' collectively blaming the Luhya community and implying they are next for unspecified consequences - ethnic targeting with an implied threat framed as retribution for their voting behaviour.

label: neither
rationale: Extended political warning addressed to 'Cousin Luos' about Ruto's manipulation of their leaders; critical of both Ruto and Luo political choices but framed as concerned advice without contempt or dehumanisation of the community.

label: offensive
rationale: 'umbwa za gachietha' - crude insults ('dogs of Gachagua') aimed at political opponents/followers of a named politician rather than an ethnic community as such.

label: neither
rationale: 'Murkomen ni kihii tu kama Kasongo' - 'kihii' is an insult aimed at named individuals (Murkomen and Kasongo) rather than their ethnic communities; 'waendee macuzo wao South Sudan' is a jibe at individuals, context is mockery of specific politicians.

label: neither
rationale: 'She is the biggest hypocrite in the history of Kenya' - personal criticism of an unnamed individual, no group characteristic.

label: offensive
rationale: 'KK criminals', 'tribalism, murder and plunder' - harsh abuse directed at named politicians (WSR, Duale) and a political faction; 'KK criminals' is ambiguous but reads as a political label for a named alliance, not ethnic contempt against a community.

label: neither
rationale: 'your people will control his presidency' - expression of political suspicion about one factional group's influence over a potential presidency; electoral coalition concern rather than ethnic contempt under NCIC standard.

label: neither
rationale: Political commentary comparing named politicians' (Gachagua, Matiangi) strategic acumen; 'kisiis' referenced in passing as part of political analysis, not targeted with contempt.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Mbwa wewe', 'disgusting kipii' - crude personal insults directed at Moses Kuria as an individual politician, no ethnic group targeted.

label: neither
rationale: Passionate call to Watanganyika (a regional political community) to rise up and remove a leader ('shetani') through protest ('maandamano'); 'mtakufa kwa njaa' is a socioeconomic prediction not a violence call, and the target of removal is a political actor not a protected group.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyu hatuna akili' - collective attribution of stupidity to the Kikuyu community as a whole, even framed self-deprecatingly, constitutes ethnic contempt under the NCIC standard.

label: offensive
rationale: 'asshole nigga', 'Moojinga' - crude personal insults aimed at Murkomen as an individual; 'I am not a Kikuyu' distances the speaker from ethnicity but the abuse targets the person, not his community.
